author: @b__h

reply_content: @b__h In semi-automated offside, the ball transmits its position 500 times per second, so the system knows the exact kick point. Broadcast video at 24–50 fps can easily freeze a frame a few milliseconds off, which is why the SAO timing wins every time. Lines drawn on broadcast feeds are much less accurate.
